articles.title: Lakers Aim to Sweep Rockets in Game 4
articles.original_title: Lakers vs. Rockets Game 4 Preview: Looking for a sweep
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers will try to close out the first‑round series against the Houston Rockets on Sunday at 6:30 p.m. PT. A sweep would be the franchise’s first in the playoffs since May 2010.
key_points: Series standing: Lakers lead 3‑0 heading into Game 4.
Game time and broadcast details: 6:30 p.m. PT, TV schedule not specified.
A sweep would mark the Lakers’ first playoff series sweep in 16 years.

key_points: Focus on the upcoming Game 4 showdown.
The preview is titled “The Dream Shake,” suggesting a spotlight on key moves or players.

key_points: Luka Dončić ruled out for Game 4.
Injury status may impact the Mavericks’ competitiveness against the Lakers.
The article does not specify the nature of the injury or expected return date.

articles.title: Lakers Defeat Rockets 107‑98 in Game 4
articles.original_title: Houston Rockets 98 - Los Angeles Lakers 107: Final score, results, recap, box score, stats - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers beat the Houston Rockets 107‑98, completing a series sweep. Yahoo Sports provides the final score, recap, box score, and statistical breakdown.
key_points: Final score: Lakers 107, Rockets 98.
Series result: Lakers win series 4‑0.
Full box score and stats are available via Yahoo Sports.

articles.title: Rockets' Depth a Glaring Problem in Game 3 Loss to Lakers
articles.original_title: Rockets' Depth a Glaring Problem in Game 3 Loss to Lakers - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: The Houston Rockets struggled with a thin bench in Game 3 against the Los Angeles Lakers, leading to a decisive defeat. Limited rotation options forced the Rockets to rely heavily on starters, exposing fatigue and defensive lapses.
key_points: Houston's bench contributed few minutes, leaving starters overextended.
Lakers capitalized on the mismatch, pulling away in the second half.
Depth concerns could affect the Rockets' ability to compete in the remainder of the series.

articles.title: Lakers Injury Report: Austin Reaves, Kevin Durant Questionable for Game 4
articles.original_title: Lakers Injury Report: Austin Reaves, Kevin Durant Questionable For Game 4
articles.summary: Ahead of Game 4 in Houston, the Lakers listed guard Austin Reaves (grade‑2 left oblique strain) and forward Kevin Durant (left ankle sprain) as questionable. Both players have missed action in the series, and their status will influence the Lakers' lineup options.
key_points: Reaves has not played in the series due to a grade‑2 oblique strain.
Durant's ankle sprain also places him on the questionable list.
The Lakers may need to adjust rotations if either player is ruled out.

articles.title: Lakers Face Pivotal Test That Could Alter Season Trajectory
articles.original_title: Lakers are headed for ultimate test that could change their entire trajectory - Lake Show Life
articles.summary: The upcoming games present a critical challenge for the Los Angeles Lakers, with the outcome potentially shaping the team's future direction. Analysts highlight the importance of execution on both ends of the floor as the Lakers seek to solidify their playoff standing.
key_points: The series is viewed as a turning point for the Lakers' postseason hopes.
Performance in the next games will influence roster decisions and strategy.
Coaching adjustments and player health are identified as key factors.

articles.title: Rich Paul Urges Lakers to Show More Gratitude for LeBron James
articles.original_title: Rich Paul Says Lakers Need To Be More Grateful For LeBron - heavy.com
articles.summary: Agent Rich Paul publicly called on the Los Angeles Lakers organization and fans to express greater appreciation for LeBron James' contributions. Paul emphasized James' leadership and impact on the franchise's success.
key_points: Paul highlighted LeBron's role in elevating the team's profile and performance.
He suggested the organization should recognize James' efforts beyond on‑court play.
The comment sparked discussion about the team's culture and player appreciation.

articles.title: Knicks Notes: Josh Hart 'locked in' defensively, OG Anunoby strong on both ends in win over Hawks
articles.original_title: Knicks Notes: Josh Hart 'locked in' defensively, OG Anunoby strong on both ends in win over Hawks
articles.summary: A few notes on the Knicks’ performance in a do-or-die Game 4 win.

articles.title: Knicks ride Karl-Anthony Towns’ rare triple-double to crucial Game 4 victory
articles.original_title: Knicks ride Karl-Anthony Towns’ rare triple-double to crucial Game 4 victory
articles.summary: Karl-Anthony Towns has long been known as one of the NBA's best-shooting big men.  On Saturday night, he showed he can be a pretty good passer, too.  Towns had the fifth triple-double in his 11-year career and first in the playoffs, leading the New York Knicks to a 114-98 win over the Atlanta Hawks to tie the first-round playoff series at two wins apiece.

articles.title: Jaden McDaniels' Late Layup Sparks Confrontation with Nikola Jokic
articles.original_title: Jaden McDaniels angers the Nuggets again with a late layup that triggers shoving from Jokic
articles.summary: In Game 4 of the first‑round series, Jaden McDaniels scored an uncontested layup in the final seconds, extending the Timberwolves' lead and sealing a win that gave Denver a 3‑1 series advantage. After the basket, Nuggets star Nikola Jokic approached McDaniels and a brief shoving incident ensued, prompting both teams' benches to intervene.
key_points: McDaniels' layup came with the game already decided, pushing the Timberwolves ahead.
Jokic confronted McDaniels, leading to a minor on‑court altercation.
The Nuggets now hold a 3‑1 lead in the series.

articles.title: Nuggets Coach Criticizes McDaniels After Late Layup Leads to Jokic Altercation
articles.original_title: Nuggets coach calls out Timberwolves' Jaden McDaniels for layup that led to altercation with Nikola Jokić
articles.summary: Following Game 4, Nuggets head coach Michael Malone publicly called out Jaden McDaniels for scoring the final basket, which he felt was unnecessary and provoked a confrontation with Nikola Jokic. Malone emphasized that the play was untimely and that the team’s focus should remain on the series, not on post‑game drama.
key_points: Malone labeled McDaniels' late scoring as “unnecessary” and “provocative.”
The coach highlighted the importance of maintaining composure as Denver eyes a series win.
The incident added tension to an already heated Nuggets‑Timberwolves rivalry.

articles.title: Ayo Dosunmu Shines as Timberwolves Fight for Survival
articles.original_title: Nuggets-Timberwolves takeaways: Ayo Dosunmu joins NBA playoff lore while Denver stares down elimination
articles.summary: Ayo Dosunmu delivered a breakout performance in Game 4, carrying the Timberwolves despite missing two of their primary contributors. His scoring burst helped keep Minnesota alive in the series, while the Nuggets remain on the brink of eliminating the Wolves.
key_points: Dosunmu scored a career‑playoff high, becoming a key offensive option.
The Timberwolves played without two of their top players, increasing Dosunmu’s impact.
Denver still leads the series 3‑1 and looks to close it out.

articles.title: Donte DiVincenzo Tears Achilles Early in Game 4, Timberwolves Hit Major Setback
articles.original_title: Donte DiVincenzo suffers torn Achilles in major playoff blow to Timberwolves
articles.summary: Less than two minutes into Game 4, Timberwolves guard Donte DiVincenzo suffered a torn Achilles tendon, ending his season. The injury further depletes Minnesota’s roster as they attempt to stave off elimination.
key_points: DiVincenzo’s injury occurred early in the game and is season‑ending.
The loss adds to the Timberwolves’ injury woes ahead of a potential Game 5.
Minnesota must rely on remaining depth, including Ayo Dosunmu, to stay competitive.

articles.title: First‑Round Playoff Winners and Losers: Dosunmu’s Surge, Brunson’s Support
articles.original_title: NBA playoffs winners and losers: Ayo Dosunmu puts the Nuggets on the brink, Jalen Brunson gets some help
articles.summary: CBS Sports recaps Saturday’s first‑round action, highlighting the Orlando Magic, Oklahoma City Thunder, New York Knicks, and Minnesota Timberwolves as the day’s winners. Ayo Dosunmu’s breakout for the Wolves and Jalen Brunson’s contributions for the Knicks were among the notable performances.
key_points: Dosunmu’s unexpected scoring burst kept the Timberwolves in the series.
Jalen Brunson received timely help from teammates, bolstering the Knicks’ effort.
The Magic, Thunder and Knicks each secured victories, while the Nuggets maintain a series lead.
